But what I’m wondering is why china doesn’t let Russia struggle in this war simply turning the ahead to the other side. Maybe china didn’t know the trump-putin-Arabs hidden plan (i doubt but I don’t know). So why helping a country in a war for the ideology egemony of the world against your future allies?(EU globalist capitalism) I’m missing this part, I’m reading a lot, informing a lot. comment.body:
One approach is to (try to) think like Trump. He just showed the world what he’s willing to do (“I’ll burn my stock market down, and I’ll do twice as bad to yours”), and he also has shown what a single tweet of his will do (“I hold all the cards”).  

And a bit cynical addendum would be that he had a few big hedge fund type donors about to blow up, and he needed to give them an out — and to mitigate problems on trading on inside information, he told them and everyone else to buy at the same time.  Or Bessent got word something in the financial plumbing was about to blow up. The world doesn’t move at his pace, so he took his foot off the gas for a bit. Pure speculation of course but let’s see what the next move is.

comment.body: My understanding is that China supports Russia to prolong the war, which in turn weakens both Russia and the West. Now you get disaccord in the West with the possibility to lure the EU, and a weakened Russia that becomes China’s source for cheap raw materials. If Russia really won the war, the alliance of convenience with China could quickly break down, as it happened in the 1960s.

comment.body: First, there is a lot of assumptions and unknowns in Almas version of what is happening. Second, all this only matters since Trump has been elected. And ideology does not explain everything. Short term they got cheap commodities for their support, but long term one of their natural antagonists (Russians hate China) is weakening himself through a miscalculated war. But they also wanted to avoid Russia collapsing because that could have meant a split of Russia into a lot of small independent oblasts/countries and with that a whole lotta instability in Central Asia, all while they are busy building their Belt and Road initiative. So there was a lot of strategic sense in the support, although they made sure to never overdo it as to become seen as a party to the war itself. Kinda like the West did with the other side.
